MT.regulatory_body: Montana Lottery (sports betting operated by Intralot under contract)
MT.secondary_regulatory_body: Montana Department of Justice, Gambling Control Division (oversees traditional gaming, NOT sports betting)
MT.archive_depth: Reports retained on site for one year. Weekly reports released every Monday, monthly reports added each month.
operators: Sports Bet Montana (sole operator, run by Intralot via Montana Lottery)

data_fields.name: Sport
data_fields.description: Sport category name

data_fields.name: Handle ($)
data_fields.description: Total dollar amount wagered on that sport

data_fields.name: Handle (%)
data_fields.description: Percentage of total handle for this sport

data_fields.name: Payout ($)
data_fields.description: Total dollar amount paid out to bettors as winnings

data_fields.name: GGR ($)
data_fields.description: Gross Gaming Revenue = Handle minus Payout

data_fields.name: Retailer Commissions
data_fields.description: Commissions paid to retail location operators (summary level)

data_fields.name: Project To Date Handle
data_fields.description: Cumulative handle since launch (3/11/2020)

data_fields.name: Project To Date Payout
data_fields.description: Cumulative payout since launch

data_fields.name: Project To Date GGR
data_fields.description: Cumulative GGR since launch

data_fields.name: Project To Date Retailer Commissions
data_fields.description: Cumulative retailer commissions since launch

MT.ggr_definition: Gross Gaming Revenue = Handle minus Payout (total wagers minus total winnings paid out)
MT.promo_deduction: Not explicitly stated in reports. Montana Lottery model - promo deductions likely handled internally by Intralot.
MT.online_tax_rate: 8.5% of adjusted gross sports betting revenue
MT.retail_tax_rate: 8.5% of adjusted gross sports betting revenue (same rate; retail kiosk-based model with mobile app requiring physical presence at licensed location)
MT.tax_basis: Adjusted gross sports betting revenue. Retailers (bars/casinos hosting kiosks) receive 6% commission on all bets placed.
MT.scraping_approach: Direct PDF download from predictable URLs. Weekly: https://sportsbetmontana.com/static/assets/Sports_Bet_Montana_Activity_WE_{date}.pdf. Monthly: https://sportsbetmontana.com/static/assets/Sports_Bet_Montana_Activity_{Month}_{Year}.pdf. PDFs are PowerPoint-converted presentations with a single table - parseable with tabula/camelot.
MT.notes: Montana operates a state-monopoly model through the Montana Lottery. Intralot is the technology vendor. Sports betting is available at ~1,800 retail kiosk locations (bars/casinos) plus a mobile app that requires physical presence at a licensed location (geofenced). No private operators allowed. Very small market (~$66M annual handle). Reports are unaudited. Weekly reports have same format as monthly. No operator breakdown needed since it's a single-operator monopoly. Sport-level breakdown is excellent with 10 categories.
MT.log: Fetched sportsbetmontana.com/en/view/news - found weekly and monthly PDF links. Downloaded January 2025 monthly and WE 1.18.25 weekly PDFs successfully. Read January 2025 PDF - confirmed sport-level breakdown with Handle/Handle%/Payout/GGR columns. DOJ Gambling Control Division site does NOT have sports betting data (covers video gambling machines only).

OR.url_status: working (press releases page). Original URL is Oregon Digital Collections - not primary source for revenue data.
OR.reporting_frequency: monthly (via press releases and third-party data aggregators); annual comprehensive financial report
OR.archive_depth: Annual comprehensive financial reports available from 2020-2025 via Oregon Secretary of State Audits Division. Monthly granular data not published as downloadable structured files.
operators: DraftKings (sole operator via Oregon Lottery Scoreboard platform)
Tribal casinos (4 retail locations: Chinook Winds, Spirit Mountain, Three Rivers, Wildhorse Resort)
sport_categories: Not publicly broken down by sport in official reports. Press releases mention football and basketball as dominant sports.

data_fields.name: Handle
data_fields.description: Total dollar amount wagered statewide

data_fields.name: Gross Gaming Revenue (GGR)
data_fields.description: Revenue retained by the sportsbook after paying out winnings

data_fields.name: Hold %
data_fields.description: GGR as percentage of handle

data_fields.name: State Tax Revenue
data_fields.description: Tax revenue generated for the state

OR.ggr_definition: Gross Gaming Revenue = total wagers minus total payouts to bettors
OR.promo_deduction: Not publicly disclosed. Oregon Lottery retains revenue share from DraftKings; promotional costs borne by DraftKings.
OR.online_tax_rate: ~8% effective rate (Oregon Lottery revenue share model - not a traditional tax but a lottery profit transfer). Revenue directed to State Lottery Fund.
OR.retail_tax_rate: Tribal casinos operate under compacts with separate revenue sharing agreements.
OR.tax_basis: Oregon Lottery profit model - lottery retains a share of sports wagering revenue. Funds directed to public education, economic development, veteran services, and responsible gambling programs.
OR.scraping_approach: No structured monthly data files available for download. Monthly data must be sourced from: (1) Oregon Lottery press releases (HTML scraping), (2) Third-party aggregators like RG.org, Covers.com, SportsBettingDime, (3) Annual Comprehensive Financial Reports (PDF, available from sos.oregon.gov/audits/). Consider using third-party data sources as primary. Official annual reports at https://sos.oregon.gov/audits/Documents/{year}-{number}.pdf
OR.scraping_time_estimate: 4-6 hours (due to lack of structured data; requires aggregation from multiple sources)
OR.notes: Oregon operates a unique state-lottery monopoly model with DraftKings as the sole technology partner for the Scoreboard platform. Monthly revenue data is released primarily through press releases and third-party tracking sites rather than structured downloadable reports. The Oregon Lottery Annual Comprehensive Financial Report (ACFR) contains detailed annual figures but not monthly breakdowns. The original URL provided (digitalcollections.library.oregon.gov) is the Oregon Digital Collections library - not the primary source for sports betting revenue data. Four tribal casinos also offer retail sports betting under separate compacts. Handle grew from $213M (2023) to $912M (2025). No sport-by-sport breakdown in public reports.
OR.log: Fetched oregonlottery.org press releases - found Super Bowl recap-style press releases, not structured data. Searched for official monthly downloads - none found. Oregon Lottery does not publish monthly structured data files. Annual ACFR available from Secretary of State. Third-party sites (RG.org, Covers.com) are the best monthly data sources.

SD.regulatory_body: South Dakota Commission on Gaming (under Department of Revenue)
SD.archive_depth: Monthly reports from 2019-present. Compiled annual statistics for 2022-2019 and historical data spanning 1989-2019. Reports retained for last 5 years at minimum.
operators: Approximately 7 Deadwood casinos reporting sports wagering revenue (individual casinos not named in public reports)
Tribal casinos may also report separately

data_fields.name: Sporting Event
data_fields.description: Name of the sport/league being wagered on

data_fields.name: Handle
data_fields.description: Total dollar amount wagered on that sporting event

data_fields.name: Statistical Win
data_fields.description: Amount won by the house (equivalent to GGR)

data_fields.name: Avg. Payout %
data_fields.description: Average percentage paid back to bettors (Handle - StatWin)/Handle

data_fields.name: Number of Casinos Reporting Revenue
data_fields.description: Count of casinos that reported sports wagering activity (summary level)

data_fields.name: Total Statistical Win (all gaming)
data_fields.description: Combined win across slots, tables, and sports

data_fields.name: Taxable Adjusted Gross Revenue
data_fields.description: Revenue after promo play and other adjustments

data_fields.name: Tax Due
data_fields.description: Total 9% tax on adjusted gross revenue

data_fields.name: Handle Comparison - % Change from Same Month Previous Year
data_fields.description: Year-over-year handle change

data_fields.name: Calendar YTD Handle
data_fields.description: Year-to-date cumulative handle

SD.ggr_definition: Statistical Win = Handle minus payouts to bettors. Equivalent to GGR. Taxable Adjusted Gross Revenue further adjusts for non-cashable promo play, promo wins, wide area progressive deductions, city slots, expired vouchers.
SD.ggr_formula: Statistical Win = Handle - Payouts. Taxable AGR = Total Statistical Win - Non Cashable Promo Play + Non Cashable Promo Wins - Wide Area Progressive Deduction - City Slots (Net of Promo) + Expired Vouchers + Audit Adjustments
SD.promo_deduction: Yes. Non-Cashable Promo Play is deducted. Non-Cashable Promo Wins are added back. Wide Area Progressive deductions also applied. Detailed in Tax Calculation section of monthly report.
SD.online_tax_rate: N/A - no statewide online sports betting. Retail only.
SD.retail_tax_rate: 9% flat on taxable adjusted gross revenue (all gaming combined, not sports-specific). Distribution: 1% to State General Fund, 8% distributed per 42-7B-48 (40% Tourism, 10% Lawrence County, 50% Commission Fund).
SD.tax_basis: Taxable Adjusted Gross Revenue across ALL gaming types (slots + tables + sports). Sports wagering is NOT taxed separately.
SD.scraping_approach: Direct PDF downloads from dor.sd.gov with somewhat predictable URLs (e.g., https://dor.sd.gov/media/fqnm5vzw/jan-2025-gaming-stats.pdf). URL slugs are not perfectly predictable - may need to scrape the gaming page first to find links. PDF format is clean tabular data, parseable with tabula. Two-page PDF: Page 1 has summary + slots + tables + sports wagering totals + handle comparison + tax calculation. Page 2 has Sports Wagering Detail with per-sport breakdown.
SD.notes: Extremely small sports betting market - one of the smallest in the US. Only retail sports betting at Deadwood casinos (7 reporting). No statewide online betting. Annual handle ~$10M (compared to billions in larger states). Sports wagering data is part of the broader Deadwood gaming statistics report - not a separate sports-specific report. The sport breakdown on page 2 is very detailed with 18 categories. Some sport categories show $0 in off-season months. Tax is calculated on ALL gaming combined, not sports-specific. The #DIV/0! errors appear in Avg. Payout % when handle is $0.
SD.log: Fetched dor.sd.gov/businesses/gaming - found monthly gaming statistics PDFs. Downloaded jan-2025-gaming-stats.pdf successfully (118KB, valid PDF). Read PDF - confirmed 2-page structure with sports wagering summary on page 1 and detailed sport-by-sport breakdown on page 2. Sports categories include 18 event types. Federal Excise Tax of $2,562.30 also noted.

DE.url_status: Original URL returned 404 (truncated URL). Correct URLs working.
DE.launch_date: 2018-06-05 (full-scale sports betting - first state post-PASPA repeal)
DE.archive_depth: Data available from 2009 to present (FY2009-FY2026). Extremely deep archive for a sports betting state.
operators: BetRivers (sole online operator, launched Dec 2023, operated by Rush Street Interactive)
sport_categories: Not broken down by sport in public reports

data_fields.name: Operator/Location
data_fields.description: Casino sportsbook name or Retailers category

data_fields.name: Number of Tickets
data_fields.description: Count of betting tickets sold

data_fields.name: Total Sports Sales
data_fields.description: Total dollar amount wagered (handle)

data_fields.name: Amount Won
data_fields.description: Total player payouts/winnings

data_fields.name: Vendor Fees
data_fields.description: Fees paid to technology vendors

data_fields.name: Net Proceeds
data_fields.description: Revenue after payouts and vendor fees

data_fields.name: State Share
data_fields.description: Portion allocated to the state

data_fields.name: Purses
data_fields.description: Portion allocated to racing purses

data_fields.name: Commission
data_fields.description: Commission payments to casino operators

DE.ggr_definition: Net Proceeds = Total Sports Sales - Amount Won - Vendor Fees. This is the Delaware equivalent of GGR after vendor costs.
DE.ggr_formula: Net Proceeds = Total Sports Sales - Amount Won - Vendor Fees
DE.promo_deduction: Not explicitly detailed in public reports. Delaware Lottery manages the sports betting program; promotional costs likely handled within the vendor fee structure.
DE.online_tax_rate: ~50% effective rate on sports betting revenue (state retains approximately 50% of net proceeds). One of the highest effective tax rates in the US.
DE.retail_tax_rate: Same ~50% effective rate. Revenue distribution: ~50% State General Fund, ~10% racing purses, remainder as commission to racinos.
DE.tax_basis: Net proceeds from sports wagering. Delaware uses a revenue-share model where the state retains a very large share of all sports betting revenue.
DE.scraping_approach: HTML tables on delottery.com organized by fiscal year. URLs follow pattern: https://www.delottery.com/Sports-Lottery/Sportsbooks/Monthly-Proceeds-And-Distribution-Financial-Year/{year}. Data split between Sportsbooks and Sports Lottery Retailers - must combine for totals. Could use requests + BeautifulSoup for HTML scraping. Archive goes back to 2009 but meaningful sports betting data starts from June 2018.
DE.notes: Delaware was the FIRST state to offer full-scale sports betting after PASPA was overturned (June 5, 2018). Unique lottery-based model with very high state revenue share (~50%). Data is split between 'Sportsbooks' (casino-based) and 'Sports Lottery Retailers' (retail tickets) - MUST combine both for complete picture. BetRivers is the only online sportsbook (launched Dec 2023). Very small market (~$140M annual handle in 2025). No sport-by-sport breakdown in public data. Archive depth back to 2009 is exceptional but pre-2018 data is for Delaware Sports Lottery (parlay cards only). Operator-level data available for the 3 casinos + retailers.
DE.log: Original URL returned 404 (truncated). Found correct URLs: Monthly-Net-Proceeds and FY-specific pages. Fetched FY2025 page - confirmed operator breakdown (Delaware Park, Bally's Dover, Harrington, Retailers) with fields: tickets, sales, amount won, vendor fees, net proceeds, state share, purses, commission. Archive goes back to 2009.

DC.regulatory_body: DC Office of Lottery and Gaming (OLG)
DC.launch_date: 2020-05-28 (GambetDC launch); 2024 (private operators approved, FanDuel replaced GambetDC)
file_formats: Image/PNG (reports posted as embedded images on web pages)
DC.archive_depth: 68 total reports available, spanning 2018-2026. Filterable by month and year.
sport_categories: Not broken down by sport in public reports

data_fields.name: Number of Bets
data_fields.description: Total number of bets/wagers accepted by operator (includes single-game bets, teasers, parlays, over-under, money line, exchange wagering, in-game wagering, proposition bets, straight bets)

data_fields.name: Handle
data_fields.description: Total dollar amount of sports wagers/bets accepted by an Operator

data_fields.name: Prizes/Payouts
data_fields.description: Cash or cash equivalents paid to customers as a result of sports wagering

data_fields.name: Gross Gaming Revenue (GGR)
data_fields.description: Total cash received from sports wagering minus total prizes/payouts

data_fields.name: Average Hold Percentage
data_fields.description: Average revenue as percentage the sportsbook retains from its handle (GGR / Handle)

data_fields.name: Tax Revenue
data_fields.description: Tax collected from operator based on class

DC.ggr_definition: Gross Gaming Revenue = Total cash or cash equivalents received from sports wagering minus the total of prizes and/or payouts.
DC.promo_deduction: Not explicitly detailed in public reports. Tax is calculated on GGR which appears to be before promotional deductions.
DC.online_tax_rate: Class A operators (DC Lottery platform): 40% of GGR. Class B operators (private venues): 20% of GGR. Class C operators: 30% of GGR.
DC.retail_tax_rate: Class B operators (private venue-based): 20% of GGR. Four stadium-based retail venues: Capital One Arena, Nationals Park, Audi Field, Northwest Stadium.
DC.tax_basis: Gross Gaming Revenue. Rates vary by operator class (A=40%, B=20%, C=30%).
DC.scraping_approach: Reports are embedded as PNG images on individual web pages - NOT machine-readable text or tables. Each month has its own page (e.g., /about-us/newsroom/dc-lottery-sports-wagering-monthly-report-{month}-{year}). Would require: (1) scraping the financials page to find all report links, (2) navigating to each monthly report page, (3) downloading the embedded PNG image, (4) using OCR (Tesseract/EasyOCR) to extract data from images. Alternatively, contact OLG directly for structured data.
DC.notes: DC has a complex multi-class operator system. GambetDC (Intralot-powered government platform) was replaced by FanDuel in 2024. Reports are published as IMAGES (PNG) embedded on web pages, making automated data extraction very challenging - requires OCR. Archive is deep (68 reports from 2018-2026). FanDuel and DraftKings account for ~80% of GGR and tax receipts. Unique tax structure with 3 different rates by operator class. Handle grew dramatically from $170M (2023) to $856M (2025) after private operators entered. No sport-by-sport breakdown.
DC.log: Fetched dclottery.com/olg/financials - confirmed 68 reports available, filterable by month/year, all marked unaudited. Found that reports are embedded PNG images, not text/tables. Searched for operator breakdown details - confirmed per-operator data exists but only in image format. Tax rates confirmed: Class A=40%, B=20%, C=30%.

AR.regulatory_body: Arkansas Racing Commission (under Department of Finance and Administration)
AR.url_status: DFA casino gaming page working. Annual report PDF URL returned HTML (may have been moved/restructured).
AR.reporting_frequency: Annual (DFA publishes Casino Gaming FYE reports). Monthly data available through third-party sources.
AR.archive_depth: Annual reports available from FYE 2019 to FYE 2026. Most recent posted 03/02/2026.
operators: DraftKings (partnered with Southland, approved Feb 2026, launched Mar 2026)
FanDuel (partnered with Oaklawn, approved Feb 2026, launched Mar 2026)
sport_categories: Not broken down by sport in public reports

data_fields.name: Taxable Gaming Revenue
data_fields.description: Combined casino and sports wagering taxable revenue (NOT broken out separately for sports)

data_fields.name: State Tax Revenue
data_fields.description: Tax collected by the state from gaming revenue

data_fields.name: Year-over-Year Change
data_fields.description: Comparison to same period in previous year

AR.ggr_definition: Net casino gaming receipts = gross receipts from casino gaming less amounts paid out or reserved as winnings to casino patrons. Sports wagering combined with casino gaming.
AR.ggr_formula: Taxable Revenue = Gross Receipts - Amounts Paid Out/Reserved as Winnings
AR.promo_deduction: Not explicitly detailed in public reports.
AR.online_tax_rate: 13% on net gaming revenue up to $150M per year per casino; 20% on revenue exceeding $150M threshold.
AR.retail_tax_rate: 13% on net gaming revenue up to $150M per year per casino; 20% on revenue exceeding $150M threshold (same rate for retail and online).
AR.tax_basis: Net casino gaming receipts. Tiered: 13% up to $150M, 20% above $150M. Applied per casino license, not statewide aggregate.
AR.scraping_approach: Official DFA data is published as annual PDF reports only - no monthly structured data files. For monthly data, must rely on: (1) DFA Monthly General Revenue Reports (aggregate tax revenue, may include gaming), (2) Third-party aggregators (RG.org, SportsBettingDime, BetArkansas.com) which compile monthly handle/GGR from DFA sources, (3) Direct FOIA request to Arkansas Racing Commission. Annual PDFs at https://www.dfa.arkansas.gov/office/taxes/excise-tax-administration/miscellaneous-tax/casino-gaming-and-sports-wagering/
AR.scraping_time_estimate: 5-7 hours (due to limited official data availability; relies on third-party sources)
AR.notes: Arkansas is very limited in public data availability. Sports betting and casino gaming revenue are combined in official reports - no separate sports wagering breakout. Only 3 casinos currently licensed (Betly/Southland, BetSaracen, Oaklawn). DraftKings and FanDuel just launched in March 2026. No individual operator revenue breakdown in public data. No sport-by-sport breakdown. Monthly data not officially published as downloadable files. The annual report URL provided returned HTML instead of PDF - may have been restructured. Through Oct 2025: $30.4M wagered on-site at 3 casinos, $477M wagered through mobile apps. Online sports wagering tax revenue was up 28.1% YoY through Oct 2025.
AR.log: Searched for DFA and Racing Commission monthly reports - found only annual Casino Gaming FYE PDF reports. Annual report PDF URL returned HTML (possible redirect/restructure). Third-party sources (RG.org, BetArkansas.com) provide monthly data compiled from DFA sources but the BetArkansas.com page is JS-rendered and inaccessible. Confirmed tiered tax rate: 13% up to $150M, 20% above.

VT.regulatory_body: Vermont Department of Liquor and Lottery
VT.url_status: returned 403 Forbidden when fetched (may require browser access or have bot protection)
VT.reporting_frequency: monthly (operators report to DLL monthly); annual report published
VT.archive_depth: Data from January 2024 (launch) to present. Annual report for FY2024 (July 2023-June 2024) available.
sport_categories: Top 5 sports reported monthly by operators
All other sports lumped into single 'Other' category

data_fields.name: Handle
data_fields.description: Total dollar amount wagered statewide

data_fields.name: Adjusted Gross Sports Wagering Revenue
data_fields.description: Revenue after payouts and adjustments

data_fields.name: Hold %
data_fields.description: Revenue as percentage of handle

data_fields.name: State Tax Revenue / Revenue Share
data_fields.description: Vermont's revenue share from operators

data_fields.name: Top 5 Sports by Handle
data_fields.description: Monthly lists of top 5 sports by total amount wagered (provided by each operator)

VT.ggr_definition: Adjusted Gross Sports Wagering Revenue = total wagers minus total payouts, with adjustments. Operators remit their contracted Vermont Revenue Share based on this calculated amount.
VT.ggr_formula: Adjusted Gross Sports Wagering Revenue = Handle - Payouts - Adjustments
VT.promo_deduction: Promotional deductions likely factored into 'Adjusted' gross revenue calculation, but specific deduction rules not publicly detailed.
VT.online_tax_rate: 31.7% operator revenue share on adjusted gross revenue (paid to state). Additionally, individual bettors pay 6% state income tax on winnings (7.25% for non-residents).
VT.retail_tax_rate: N/A - Vermont is online-only, no retail sportsbooks.
VT.tax_basis: Adjusted Gross Sports Wagering Revenue. Operator fees: $550,000 per operator (paid once every 3 years). FY24 operator fees totaled $1,650,000.
VT.scraping_approach: Official website returned 403 Forbidden. Monthly data not publicly available as structured downloads. Options: (1) Annual report PDF from Vermont Legislature documents (available), (2) Third-party aggregators (RG.org, Covers.com) for monthly data, (3) FOIA request to DLL for monthly structured data. The DLL collects 'very limited data' per reporting. Annual report at https://legislature.vermont.gov/Documents/2026/Workgroups/Senate%20Economic%20Development/Reports%20and%20Resources/W~Department%20of%20Liquor%20and%20Lottery~Annual%20Report~5-27-2025.pdf
VT.scraping_time_estimate: 5-7 hours (website has bot protection; limited official data availability)
VT.notes: Vermont just launched January 11, 2024 - one of the newest sports betting states. Online-only model with 3 operators (FanDuel, DraftKings, Fanatics). The Department of Liquor and Lottery 'collects very limited data' per reporting. Sport breakdown is LIMITED: operators provide monthly top-5 sports by handle, everything else lumped into 'Other'. No individual operator revenue breakdown in public data. FY24 revenue (Jan-Jul 2024): $3.53M. Full year 2024: ~$198.8M handle. Full year 2025: ~$235.1M handle. Vermont prohibits individual player prop bets on NCAA athletes (one of first states to do so). The 31.7% revenue share rate is among the highest in the US. Individual bettors also taxed at 6%. Revenue below initial projections.
VT.log: liquorandlottery.vermont.gov returned 403 Forbidden. Downloaded annual report PDF from Vermont Legislature documents (1.2MB, valid PDF). Read pages 1-15 - confirmed FY2024 report covering Jul 2023-Jun 2024. Sports Wagering section on page 12: FanDuel, DraftKings, Fanatics selected. Basketball highest handle ($40.2M FY24), Baseball second ($9.4M). FY24 revenue $3.53M, operator fees $1.65M. Searched for monthly structured data - not available officially.

MS.archive_depth: Monthly reports archived by year. Current year and previous years available. Charitable gaming data goes back to 1997. Sports wagering reports available from 2018 to present.
operators: Pearl River Sports (Choctaw Casinos, on-premises mobile)
Multiple casino-based retail sportsbooks across 3 regions

data_fields.name: Region
data_fields.description: Geographic region of casinos (Central, Coastal, Northern)

data_fields.name: Sports Type
data_fields.description: Category of sport wagered on

data_fields.name: Write*
data_fields.description: Total dollar amount wagered (handle). Uses modified accrual basis calculation.

data_fields.name: Taxable Revenue*
data_fields.description: Revenue subject to tax. Uses modified accrual basis calculation. Can be negative if payouts exceed wagers.

data_fields.name: Win %
data_fields.description: Taxable Revenue as percentage of Write (equivalent to hold rate)

data_fields.name: % Change**
data_fields.description: Current month compared to previous year same month

data_fields.name: Overall for Region
data_fields.description: Subtotal for each geographic region

data_fields.name: Overall for State
data_fields.description: Statewide total (on final page/region)

MS.ggr_definition: Taxable Revenue = Write (handle) minus payouts. Uses modified accrual basis calculation. Can be negative when payouts exceed wagers for a given sport/period.
MS.ggr_formula: Taxable Revenue = Write - Payouts (modified accrual basis)
MS.promo_deduction: Not explicitly detailed in sports wagering reports. Modified accrual basis calculation may incorporate some adjustments.
MS.online_tax_rate: N/A currently - no statewide mobile sports betting. On-premises mobile only. Proposed: 22% on mobile sports wagering revenue (House Bill 4074, pending Senate approval as of March 2026).
MS.retail_tax_rate: 12% total: 8% to state + 4% to local jurisdictions
MS.tax_basis: Taxable Revenue (modified accrual basis). Revenue derived from Mississippi Department of Revenue calculations.
MS.scraping_approach: Monthly reports available in BOTH PDF and Excel formats - Excel is ideal for automated ingestion. URLs follow pattern: https://www.msgamingcommission.com/files/monthly_reports/{MMYY}_sports_wagering.{pdf|xlsx}. Note: Direct curl downloads were blocked (returned HTML) - may require browser-based download or session cookies. Monthly report detail pages at https://www.msgamingcommission.com/reports/monthly_details/{id}. Alternative: Use Selenium/Playwright to navigate to monthly_details pages and click download links.
MS.notes: Mississippi has retail-only sports betting at licensed casinos. Mobile wagering is restricted to on-premises (within casino property). Legislature considering statewide mobile betting (HB 4074 passed House with 22% tax rate, pending Senate). Reports have excellent structure: 3 pages, one per region (Central, Coastal, Northern), with statewide total on the Northern page. Sport categories: Football, Basketball, Baseball, Parlay Cards, Other. Excel format available which is excellent for automated ingestion. Data uses 'Write' terminology instead of 'Handle'. Modified accrual basis. Taxable Revenue can be NEGATIVE (e.g., Football in Central region June 2024: -$10,275.40). Historical archive back to 2018 launch. Annual handle peaked at $586M (2021), declined to ~$452M by 2024.
MS.log: Fetched msgamingcommission.com/reports/monthly_reports - confirmed PDF + Excel formats available. Fetched monthly_details/1166 (June 2024) - got exact download URLs. Direct curl download returned HTML (blocked). Used browser user-agent to download PDF successfully (3 pages). Read PDF - confirmed 3-region structure with sport-by-sport breakdown. Fields: Sports Type, Write, Taxable Revenue, Win %, % Change.

Douglas County South Shore Lake Tahoe
Washoe County (Reno, Sparks, North Shore Lake Tahoe, Balance)

data_fields.description: Average units over 12-month period

data_fields.name: Win** Amount (12 Month)
data_fields.description: Win amounts in thousands for 12-month period

data_fields.description: 12-month YoY change

data_fields.name: Win Percent (12 Month)
data_fields.description: 12-month win percentage

NV.ggr_definition: Win = amount won by the house (casino/sportsbook), equivalent to GGR. For games, Win Percent is adjusted for effects of credit play. For slot devices, Win Percent is reported win amount divided by total dollar amount played by patrons. Win Amounts are in THOUSANDS.
NV.ggr_formula: Win = Total Wagers - Total Payouts (reported in thousands). Handle is NOT directly reported - only Win amounts.
NV.promo_deduction: Not separately broken out in public GRI reports. Taxable gaming revenue data is provided in an appendix (page A-01). Licensees file Monthly Gross Revenue Statistical Reports (Form NGC-31).
NV.online_tax_rate: 6.75% of gross gaming revenue (one of the lowest rates in the US). Same rate for mobile and retail.
NV.retail_tax_rate: 6.75% of gross gaming revenue (same rate for all nonrestricted licensees).
NV.tax_basis: Gross gaming revenue. Taxable revenue data in Appendix A-1 of monthly report. All gaming types taxed at same rate.
NV.scraping_approach: PDF reports with consistent structure but dense tabular data across 45+ pages. URLs follow pattern: https://www.gaming.nv.gov/siteassets/content/about/gaming-revenue/{year}{month_abbr}-gri.pdf (older format) or https://www.gaming.nv.gov/contentassets/{hash}/{month}-{year}-monthly-revenue-report.pdf (newer format). URL patterns have changed over time. Need to scrape the GRI page for links or use both URL patterns. PDF parsing with tabula - tables have complex multi-column headers (1-month, 3-month, 12-month periods). Sports data is EMBEDDED within the full gaming report - must extract specific rows. Mobile Sports shown separately. Geographic breakdown across 20+ areas means data is repeated on many pages.
NV.scraping_time_estimate: 6-8 hours (complex PDF structure, 45+ pages, need to extract sports rows from larger gaming report)
NV.notes: Nevada is the 'granddaddy' of US sports betting (legal since 1949). Sports betting data is EMBEDDED within the comprehensive Gaming Revenue Information (GRI) report that covers ALL gaming types (slots, table games, race book, sports). Sports wagering is NOT reported in a separate document. Win amounts are reported in THOUSANDS of dollars. Handle is NOT directly reported - only Win (GGR) amounts. Mobile Sports is shown as a SEPARATE line item aggregating all sports for personal device wagering. Unit detail shown separately only when 3+ licensed locations report; otherwise included in 'Other'. Geographic breakdown is extremely detailed (20+ areas across counties). The report is based on Form NGC-31 filed by nonrestricted licensees. Restricted licensees NOT included. Data subject to change due to late filings, reports, audits, and adjustments. Report is ~48 pages. Annual handle ~$8B with ~$600M GGR. 6.75% tax rate is among the lowest in the US.
NV.log: Fetched gaming.nv.gov/about/gaming-revenue/information - confirmed monthly PDF reports from 2004-present. Downloaded 2025oct-gri.pdf (465KB, 48 pages). Read pages 1-5 - confirmed structure: cover page, introduction, table of contents, then data pages. Statewide page shows sports categories: Race Book Parimutuel, Sports - Mobile, Sports - Football, Sports - Basketball, Sports - Baseball, Sports Parlay Cards, Sports - Hockey, Other. Data in 1-month/3-month/12-month columns with # locations, # units, Win amount (thousands), % change, Win percent. Detailed geographic breakdown across multiple pages.
